name: Melissa Bean
bio: Melissa Bean is a former U.S. Representative for Illinois' 8th Congressional District, serving from 2005 to 2011, when she was defeated in the Tea Party wave election. During her tenure she was known as a moderate Democrat, serving on the House Financial Services Committee. She has worked in the financial services and technology industries since leaving Congress. She re-entered politics to seek the open seat created by Raja Krishnamoorthi's Senate run and has emerged as a frontrunner with significant fundraising support.

news_hits.title: Editorial: Melissa Bean and Jennifer Davis for the 8th Congressional District primary
news_hits.summary: Chicago Tribune editorial board endorses Melissa Bean for the Democratic nomination to succeed Rep. Raja Krishnamoorthi in IL-08, citing her prior experience as a former U.S. Representative for the district from 2005–2011.

news_hits.title: At the Races: Big primaries in the Prairie State
news_hits.summary: Roll Call Election Day roundup names Bean as one of eight Democrats vying to succeed Krishnamoorthi in IL-08. Other notable candidates in the field include Melissa Conyears-Ervin, Kina Collins, La Shawn Ford, and Jason Friedman.

news_hits.title: Early front-runner Melissa Bean seeks to reclaim 8th Congressional District seat she lost in 2010
news_hits.summary: Melissa Bean is attempting a political comeback in IL-08, the seat she held 2005-2011. Voters decide today which Democratic hopeful will be on the November ballot.

news_hits.title: Illinois Eighth Congressional District Primary 2026: Live Election Results
news_hits.summary: Live primary results for the crowded IL-08 Democratic primary; Melissa Bean among prominent candidates with Junaid Ahmed, Kevin Morrison, and Yasmeen Bankole.

news_hits.title: Illinois primary: Melissa Bean wins 8th District Democratic race
news_hits.summary: Tribune confirms Bean declared victory in the 8th Congressional District Democratic primary. She will advance to the November general election to reclaim the seat she held from 2005-2011 before losing to GOP's Bob Dold.

news_hits.title: Illinois Primary Election Takeaways: Stratton Wins and AIPAC's Power Is Tested
news_hits.summary: NYT takeaways: Bean's 8th District win is cited as one of AIPAC's successful investments in the Illinois primary — her $7.2M in super PAC backing was the highest of any candidate in the state. She advances to a November general election in a competitive suburban seat.

news_hits.title: Melissa Bean likely heading back to Congress after Illinois primary win
news_hits.summary: Roll Call reports former Rep. Melissa Bean won the Democratic primary for Illinois' 8th District, putting her on track to likely return to Congress. Bean defeated challengers Junaid Ahmed, Yasmeen Bankole, and others in the crowded field vacated by Raja Krishnamoorthi's Senate run. The NYT noted her win represents a triumph for big-money outside groups that spent heavily in Illinois.

news_hits.title: AIPAC Spent $12 Million Losing Illinois Primary Elections — But Bean Won in the 8th
news_hits.summary: Newsweek post-primary analysis of AIPAC's $12M overall spending across Illinois. Elect Chicago Women (AIPAC-aligned PAC) spent ~$3.9M supporting Bean in the 8th Congressional District, and Bean won — making her one of the few AIPAC-backed candidates to prevail in a cycle where the group's candidates lost in multiple high-profile races including IL-07 (Conyears-Ervin) and the Senate race. Bean defeated a crowded field including Junaid Ahmed, Yasmeen Bankole, and others.

news_hits.title: Super PAC scorecard — how outside spending groups fared in efforts to influence Illinois primary voters
news_hits.summary: WBEZ's definitive post-primary spending breakdown: Elect Chicago Women, an AIPAC-aligned PAC, spent $3.9 million supporting Melissa Bean in the 8th Congressional District primary. Bean won, becoming one of the few AIPAC-backed candidates to prevail on a night when the pro-Israel lobby's $12M-plus outlay yielded mixed results statewide.

news_hits.title: AIPAC Spent $12 Million Losing Illinois Primary Elections — Except Where It Backed Melissa Bean
news_hits.summary: Analysis of AIPAC's $12M spend in Illinois primaries: despite major losses on Senate and other races, AIPAC-backed Melissa Bean won the IL-08 primary, making Bean's race a rare win for the pro-Israel PAC.

news_hits.title: 2026 primary day election results: 8th Congressional District of Illinois
news_hits.summary: Chicago Tribune live primary results page for Illinois's 8th Congressional District Democratic primary. With Rep. Raja Krishnamoorthi running for U.S. Senate, a crowded field emerged; Melissa Bean wins the Democratic nomination and will face Republican Jennifer Davis in the November general election.

news_hits.title: More than $30M in outside spending shaped Illinois primaries, but wins were a split for big-monied interests
news_hits.summary: Post-primary analysis covers Bean's IL-08 win as one of the AIPAC-aligned victories in Illinois. Miller and Bean — both supported by Elect Chicago Women super PAC ($3.9M for Bean) — won their primaries, while the AIPAC-aligned candidate Conyears-Ervin lost in the 7th District. Bean's win is cited as a partial success for the pro-Israel spending coalition.

news_hits.title: AIPAC, AI money propels Melissa Bean to comeback victory in Illinois
news_hits.summary: Politico reports Melissa Bean's primary win was powered in part by AIPAC and tech/AI-aligned PAC spending in the crowded IL-08 Democratic field. Bean, a former moderate Democrat who served 2005-2011, is now poised to return to Congress in a seat considered safely Democratic in November.

news_hits.title: Former congresswoman Melissa Bean wins primary to take back former suburban Chicago seat
news_hits.summary: Fox News reports Melissa Bean, the former congresswoman who represented Illinois' 8th Congressional District before losing her seat in 2010, has won Tuesday's primary election to retake the seat. Bean ran in a significantly redrawn 8th District following Krishnamoorthi's Senate run.

news_hits.title: Melissa Bean wins 8th Congressional District primary, moves to reclaim seat she lost 16 years ago
news_hits.summary: WBEZ reports AP declared Bean the winner shortly after 9:15 p.m. on March 17, with Bean leading the 8-candidate field with 32% of the vote at 91% counted. Bean, backed by more than $7 million in outside super PAC spending, won the Democratic primary to succeed Rep. Raja Krishnamoorthi in Illinois' northwest suburban 8th District — 16 years after she lost the seat to Republican Bob Dold in 2010.

news_hits.title: Former congresswoman Melissa Bean wins Democratic nomination, advances to face Republican Jennifer Davis in IL-08
news_hits.summary: AP News reports former U.S. Rep. Melissa Bean won the Democratic nomination for Illinois' open 8th Congressional District seat (vacated by Raja Krishnamoorthi), and advances to face Republican Jennifer Davis in November in the suburban Chicago district.

news_hits.title: Illinois primary election 2026: Melissa Bean projected to win Democratic nomination for 8th Congressional District seat
news_hits.summary: ABC7 Chicago reports Melissa Bean is projected to win the Democratic nomination for Illinois' 8th Congressional District, reclaiming the seat she held from 2005-2011. Jennifer Davis is projected to win the Republican nomination. Bean led a crowded 8-candidate field with 32% at 93% counted. General election matchup: Bean (D) vs. Davis (R) — a district that has not sent a Republican to Congress since Davis's predecessor Bob Dold won in 2010.

news_hits.title: Melissa Bean projected to win 2026 Democratic primary in Illinois 8th Congressional District
news_hits.summary: CBS Chicago projects Melissa Bean wins the Democratic primary for Illinois' open 8th Congressional District. On the Republican side, software company owner Jennifer Davis led with 51.2% at 69% counted. Bean will face Davis in November's general election in the suburban Chicago district that has leaned Democratic since 2012.
